author: Dr. Olivia Reyes (Emergency Medicine)
content:
ED RE-PRESENTATION H&P — UNIVERSITY OF MARYLAND MEDICAL CENTER
Date/Time: 8/10/2027, 2:00 PM (ED arrival ~10:45 AM).

CHIEF COMPLAINT: Recurrent shortness of breath and pleuritic chest pain.

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr. Bell is the 43-year-old man with recent (7/2027) unprovoked PE + extensive DVT and a liver mass concerning for malignancy, discharged on apixaban. He presented today to interventional radiology for his scheduled outpatient liver biopsy. Per IR instructions, his apixaban had been HELD for the prior 3 days in preparation for the procedure. On arrival to the appointment he reported recurrent dyspnea and sharp pleuritic chest pain that had developed over the preceding day. He was sent to the ED and the biopsy was cancelled.

ED COURSE / VITALS: HR 115 (sinus tachycardia), BP 118/74, RR 24, SpO2 93% on 4 L nasal cannula, afebrile.
- CTA chest (IMG006): pulmonary embolism now in BOTH main pulmonary arteries — PROGRESSED from the prior segmental clot; RV:LV ratio increased.
- Troponin (LAB004): NEWLY ELEVATED (0.18). BNP elevated.
- TTE (IMG007): NEW right ventricular strain (dilation, hypokinesis, septal flattening) — not present on the prior study.
- Lower-extremity duplex (IMG008): persistent right-leg DVT.
- Lactate normal; blood pressure maintained.
- A heparin infusion was started in the ED.

EXAM: Tachypneic, mild distress, on nasal cannula. Tachycardic, regular. Lungs clear. Right calf with residual fullness. No bleeding.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
43M with RECURRENT / PROGRESSED bilateral PE and NEW right-heart strain (intermediate-risk / submassive). Hemodynamically stable (normotensive, normal lactate).
#Restart therapeutic anticoagulation — heparin infusion (done).
#Admit to medicine/cardiology for monitoring and management of intermediate-risk PE.
#Liver biopsy cancelled; timing/strategy to be revisited.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Olivia Reyes, MD (Emergency Medicine). 8/10/2027.
